verdict_summary: RRN decode absent (FIX-F suppress preserved) but only English 'Something went wrong' fallback rendered — FIX-G signalR envelope inject did not engage; same outcome as #757.

evidence.console_errors_excerpt: WebSocket connection to 'wss://substrate.office.com/m365Copilot/Chathub/6f30b2b...' failed; Uncaught (in promise) Error: Error parsing handshake response: Error: Expected a handshake response from the server (at g._processHandshakeResponse, g._processIncomingData, connection.onreceive, i.onmessage); GET https://res-1.cdn.office.net/midgard-bootstrapper/versionless/livep... net::ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR; Error during chat: Error: Error parsing handshake response

recommendation: FIX-G inject path did not engage. The WebSocket failed at handshake parse stage (Error parsing handshake response) — this means no WS frames were received post-domain-block-detection, so the ws_inject_count==0 condition for envelope injection was never reached. Engine path falls back to FIX-F suppress only, yielding the generic English 'Something went wrong' UI. Recommend: (1) verify [APF:ws_domain_block_inject] log emission on engine side to confirm whether inject was attempted; (2) consider injecting at handshake-response synthesis stage rather than waiting for first post-block frame, since handshake itself fails when WS is suppressed; (3) alternative: inject via HTTP response path (similar to other services) since WS is non-functional in this blocked state.
notes: User prompt rendered correctly in chat. Copilot bubble shows ONLY 'Something went wrong. Please try again later.' followed by 다시 시도 / 피드백 보내기 buttons. No Korean text indicating APF warning anywhere. No structural RRN decode (no YYMMDD parsing, no gender/region info, no birthdate analysis). FIX-F suppress mechanism appears to still be working (no leak), but FIX-G enhancement did not deliver Korean warning. Mission target OK_S2_AND_warning NOT achieved — same outcome as #757. WS state shows ws connection failure at handshake stage, indicating the engine likely closed/blocked the WS before the inject branch could fire on a real frame.
